---
title: "Project Setup"
output: html_document
---



```{r}
# 1. Configuration & Detection
os <- Sys.info()[["sysname"]]

check_java_version <- function(path = "java") {
  # Runs 'java -version' on a specific path or the default 'java'
  cmd <- if(path == "java") "java" else file.path(path, "bin", "java")
  
  ver_out <- tryCatch(
    system2(cmd, "-version", stdout = TRUE, stderr = TRUE),
    error = function(e) return(NULL)
  )
  if (is.null(ver_out) || length(ver_out) == 0) return("None")
  return(ver_out[1])
}

# Check what the system is currently using
current_system_java <- check_java_version()
is_already_on_17 <- grepl("17\\.", current_system_java)

# 2. The "Strict" Discovery Function
detect_java_17 <- function() {
  found_path <- NULL
  
  if (os == "Darwin") {
    # Check if java_home can actually find 17
    # Use '2>/dev/null' to suppress the fallback behavior in some shell environments
    found_path <- tryCatch(
      system("/usr/libexec/java_home -v 17 2>/dev/null", intern = TRUE),
      error = function(e) return(NULL)
    )
  } else if (os == "Windows") {
    # 1. Check common parent directories
    possible_roots <- c("C:/Program Files/Java", "C:/Program Files/Microsoft/jdk")
    
    for (root in possible_roots) {
      if (dir.exists(root)) {
        # Look for any folder starting with 'jdk-17'
        subdirs <- list.dirs(root, recursive = FALSE, full.names = TRUE)
        match <- subdirs[grepl("jdk-17", basename(subdirs))]
        
        if (length(match) > 0) {
          # Use the first match found
          found_path <- match[1]
          break
        }
      }
    }
  } else {
    p <- "/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64"
    if (dir.exists(p)) found_path <- p
  }
  
  # VALIDATION: Only return the path if it actually leads to Java 17
  if (!is.null(found_path) && length(found_path) > 0) {
    # On Mac, java_home returns the full path including /Contents/Home
    if (grepl("17", found_path)) return(found_path)
  }
  return(NULL)
}

# 3. Decision Logic
java_17_path <- detect_java_17()

cat("--- JAVA DIAGNOSTIC ---\n")
cat("Operating System:", os, "\n")
cat("System Default:  ", current_system_java, "\n")

if (!is.null(java_17_path)) {
  # found 17 somewhere, even if it's not the system default
  Sys.setenv(JAVA_HOME = java_17_path)
  
  java_bin <- file.path(java_17_path, "bin")
  old_path <- Sys.getenv("PATH")
  Sys.setenv(PATH = paste(java_bin, old_path, sep = ";"))
  cat("SUCCESS: Java 17 found and JAVA_HOME set to:\n", java_17_path, "\n")
  
  if (!is_already_on_17) {
    cat("Note: Your system default is different, but Spark will use Java 17.\n")
  }
  
} else {
  # cannot find Java 17 anywhere
  cat("ERROR: Java 17 not found on this machine.\n\n")
  cat("Attempting to install...\n")
  
  if (os == "Darwin") {
      system("brew install openjdk@17")
      
      #This step must be performed by the user because of sudo permissions
      cat("\nSuccess! Now, please copy-paste this into your Terminal to finish:\n")
      cat("sudo ln -sfn /opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@17/libexec/openjdk.jdk /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk-17.jdk\n")
      
    } else if (os == "Windows") {
      system("winget install Oracle.JDK.17")
      
    } else {
      #This step must be performed by the user because of sudo permissions
      cat("Please run: sudo apt install openjdk-17-jdk\n")
    }
  
  
}
  
  

cat("\nFor Confirmation:\n")
system("java -version")
```

```{r}
#Install required libraries and instantiate them in environment
if (!require(aws.s3)) install.packages("aws.s3") else message("S3 installed previously.")
if (!require(sparklyr)) install.packages("sparklyr") else message("SparklyR installed previously.")
if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr") else message("dplyr installed previously.")
if (!require(DBI)) install.packages("DBI") else message("DBI installed previously.")


library(aws.s3)
library(sparklyr)
library(dplyr)
library(DBI)
message("Libraries successfully instantiated.")
```

```{r}
if (nrow(spark_installed_versions() |> dplyr::filter(spark == "3.5.0")) == 0) {
  spark_install(version = "3.5.0")
    message("Spark 3.5 has been installed.")
} else {
  message("Spark 3.5 is already installed.")
}
```
```{r}
#remove all setup variables
rm(list = ls())
```
